Get Started — Develop

Code Branching Guidelines

Naming conventions and compliance standards for managing Git branches across all NESV repositories.

Repository Compliance

To accurately measure compliance requirements, all repositories are assigned one of three statuses.

Compliant

New repositories start as Compliant as long as they adhere consistently to this convention. Compliant status is lost when a repeated violation occurs.

Partially Compliant

Existing repositories can attain Partially Compliant status after following the convention over a reasonable amount of time.

Non-Compliant

All existing repositories start with Non-Compliant status by default until they demonstrate adherence to the convention.

Branch Types

Main Branch

Development

development

The main branch for all changes. All Change branches must originate from here. Major and Minor Change branches merge back into this branch once QA is cleared.

Change Branch

Client Change Branch

CC-*

Contains changes requested by a client — new features and/or bug reports.

Can branch off when necessary for:
  • Test result changes from the team
  • Additional changes scoped-in
Change Branch

Internal Change Branch

IC-*

Contains changes reported by the internal team. These changes should only contain bug fixes and feature corrections.

Can branch off when necessary for:
  • Test result changes from the team

Naming Convention

1

Development branch

Uses the name development.

Branch development
2

Feature Main Branch

Uses the prefix CC followed by a dash and the 4-digit version number with periods replaced by underscores.

Format CC-[major]_[minor]_[release]_[build]
Example A major change request with version 2.2.0 → CC-2_2_0_0
3

Change Branch (Client)

Prefixed with CC. The second part is the 3-digit version with periods replaced by underscores.

Format CC-[major]_[minor]_[release]
Example Client change request v2.2.0 → CC-2_2_0
4

Change Branch (Internal)

Prefixed with IC. The second part is the 3-digit version with periods replaced by underscores.

Format IC-[major]_[minor]_[release]
Example Internal QA change request v2.2.1 → CC-2_2_1

Branch Rules

Major and Minor releases are the changes that shall branch out from development.

Build and Release branches must branch out from Major and Minor branches.

All completed individual branches by developers must merge back to Major and Minor branches.

QA shall be performed in the Major or Minor branch.

Once QA is cleared, Major and Minor branches can merge back to development.

Branch Flow
development
CC / IC (Major & Minor)
Build / Release branches
After QA
development
On this page
